what_it_does: Builds a navigable compaction timeline from a Claude Code JSONL session transcript and materializes a new resumable session file from a chosen historical point by rewriting the source prefix's sessionId and uuids, adding forkedFrom provenance, and verifying structural integrity without spawning Claude.
observations: The module is driven by a long design docstring that states capability, mechanism, laws, point grammar, and verification scope.
It parses three point specifiers: compact:N, uuid:<uuid>, and ts:<iso8601>.
build_timeline scans JSONL once, tracking compact_boundary events, summary heads, preserved runs, and duplicate payload copies.
materialize_at_point performs a read-only source scan, deterministic uuid5 remapping, atomic temp write, and a before/after source stat check.

verify_materialized checks that every line parses, the tip is the remapped cut event, no stale identifiers remain outside forkedFrom, and the parentUuid chain walks to a root using the latest earlier occurrence.
encode_project_dir and resume_cwd_for map a cwd to Claude Code's encoded project directory name and resolve the correct resume directory.
materialized_registry_record produces a schema-additive registry record with provenance fields.
standouts: Deterministic uuid5 remap keyed by new session id makes re-materialization idempotent-by-content.
Chain-walk verification handles duplicate uuids from re-appended preserved runs by resolving parents to the latest earlier occurrence.
Source-untouched proof via stat size and mtime_ns before and after materialization.
Project-directory encoding and candidate validation to avoid spawning resume in the wrong cwd.
conventions: Type hints and detailed docstrings on public functions.
JSONL parsing tolerates torn lines and counts them rather than failing.
Atomic file creation via tempfile in the destination directory followed by os.replace.
concerns: The before/after stat check will frequently fail for live sessions because their mtime/size change continuously, and the code raises a hard error even though it acknowledges the race.
verify_materialized relies on re-scanning the source prefix for old uuids, so if the source stat check were ever relaxed the stale sweep could become inconsistent.
resume_cwd_for's naive dash-to-slash decode can produce paths that happen to exist but are not the original cwd; it mitigates this by re-encoding, but ambiguity remains.
forkedFrom is injected into every event that has a type field, not only the first line, which may be more invasive than the native fork stamp shape.
notes: A careful, docstring-heavy materializer that turns a historical transcript prefix into a new resumable session file while trying to prove it never touched the original.
questions: How does the supervisor actually invoke claude --resume with the new_sid and resolved cwd?
What is the exact schema for compactMetadata and preservedMessages across all Claude Code versions?
Why is forkedFrom added to every event rather than a single boundary/system event?
What happens when the source stat check fails mid-materialization for a genuinely live session?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds timelines and materializes point-in-time session files using the native-fork transform on a prefix.
intention_signals: To provide a documented, non-wrapper path for resuming a Claude Code session at a past moment by treating the transcript prefix as that moment's actual resumable state and applying the same transform the SDK uses for forks.
novelty: It applies the native fork transform to a transcript prefix rather than a full session, producing a new file that stock Claude Code can resume without any wrapper around the runtime.
contribution: A self-contained point-in-time materializer that indexes compaction boundaries, remaps identities deterministically, verifies the resulting transcript's chain and identifiers, and emits a registry record with provenance.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a point-in-time session materializer that creates a new resumable Claude Code session transcript from a prefix of an existing session's JSONL transcript at a specified compaction, uuid, or timestamp boundary.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to enable launching or consulting a past Claude Code session at a chosen historical moment while preserving the native resume path and proving structural correctness without spawning Claude.

symbols.does: Teaching refusal exception for invalid point specs or resolution failures.
symbols.description: A ValueError subclass used throughout the module to raise loud, instructive errors that name what exists and how to ask correctly, rather than exposing bare stack traces.

symbols.does: Parses the compact:N, uuid:<uuid>, or ts:<iso> point mini-language into a dict.
symbols.description: Validates the at= string, rejects empty or unknown grammars, normalizes uuids to lowercase, and ensures ISO-8601 timestamps carry a timezone before returning a structured point descriptor.

symbols.does: Streams a JSONL file yielding line numbers and parsed objects, tolerating torn lines.
symbols.description: A generator that reads the transcript line by line, skipping blank lines and returning None for malformed JSON so callers can count bad lines instead of crashing on historical corpus damage.

symbols.does: Builds a navigable compaction timeline from a single streaming pass over the transcript.
symbols.description: Identifies compact_boundary events, tracks their summary heads and preserved runs, deduplicates payload copies of earlier boundaries, and returns boundary and segment metadata including launchable resume_cut_line and resume_cut_uuid.

symbols.does: Closes the current inter-boundary segment and appends it to the segment list.
symbols.description: An inner helper used by build_timeline to finalize a segment's end line and store a copy of the running segment dictionary.

symbols.does: Resolves a parsed point to a cut line, uuid, and timestamp in the transcript.
symbols.description: Looks up compact points against the timeline, scans the file for uuid points, and finds the last user/assistant event at or before a timestamp for ts points, raising teaching errors on misses.

symbols.does: Recursively rewrites full-field string values that match an identity mapping.
symbols.description: Walks a decoded event and replaces any string whose entire value appears in the mapping, ensuring uuids and sessionIds are remapped without altering uuids embedded inside prose.

symbols.does: Writes a new resumable session file from the source prefix at the chosen point.
symbols.description: Determines the cut, builds a deterministic uuid5 mapping, writes the prefix to a temp file in the same directory with rewritten identities and forkedFrom provenance, atomically renames it, and proves the source was untouched via stat checks.

symbols.does: Structurally verifies a materialized file without spawning Claude.
symbols.description: Checks line counts, that the tip equals the remapped cut event, sweeps for stale source sessionId or old uuids outside forkedFrom, and walks the parentUuid chain to a root using the latest earlier occurrence to handle duplicate uuids.

symbols.does: Recursively scans an event for stale source identifiers.
symbols.description: An inner helper that traverses dicts, lists, and strings, flagging any full-field value equal to the source sessionId or an old prefix uuid while deliberately skipping the forkedFrom provenance field.

symbols.does: Encodes a cwd into Claude Code's project directory name.
symbols.description: Replaces slash and dot with dash, matching the observed ~/.claude/projects/<encoded> naming so a transcript can be located by its project dir.

symbols.does: Resolves the cwd required for claude --resume to find the transcript.
symbols.description: Validates candidate cwds by re-encoding them, falls back to a naive dash-to-slash decode only if it exists and re-encodes correctly, and refuses loudly rather than spawning in the wrong directory.

symbols.does: Produces a registry record for a materialized session.
symbols.description: Builds a schema-additive record from the materialization report and optional source record, carrying provenance fields such as materialized_from, materialized_at_point, and materialized_cut_uuid so the registry remains the source of truth.
